MARETO HQ  —  USER GUIDE
The Person Module
Searching, Creating & Managing Person Records

What is the Person Module?

The Person Module is a core module shared across all programs in your organization. This is by design — it ensures that client data is never fragmented across your system.

If a client has been a participant in one program and later reaches out for services again, or needs services from multiple programs at the same time, you do not need to create a second record. Instead, you use the same person record so your organization maintains a single source of truth.

Why this matters:

When a client updates their contact information with one program, that change is automatically reflected across all programs. There is only one place to update information, keeping everything accurate and consistent for all staff.

The Person Registry

The Person Registry is where all person records in your organization are stored. When you open it, you will see an overview dashboard with summary statistics, including total person records, a breakdown by role, and a breakdown by Indigenous identification.

The Person Registry overview with record statistics and visual breakdowns.

Below the overview, you will find the full list of person records. The registry includes both Active Records and Inactive Records, which can be toggled using the tabs above the list.

The Active Records list in the Person Registry.

Searching for an Existing Person

The first step any time a new client is identified is to search the Person Registry to check whether that individual already exists in your system. This is critical for preventing duplicate records.

Step 1   Open the Person Registry

Click Person Registry in the left-hand navigation menu.

Step 2   Use the Search Bar

Enter the client’s name or identifying information into the search bar. You can search in a variety of ways:

  • By first name
  • By last name
  • By last name, first name (e.g. Croft, Nicole)
  • By date of birth
  • By alias or other known names

Searching for a person by typing their name into the search bar.

Step 3   Check Both Active and Inactive Records

Make sure you check both the Active Records and Inactive Records tabs to see if that participant already exists. This helps limit duplications in your system.

Switching to the Inactive Records tab to check for existing records.

Tip:

Always search before creating a new record. A client may have been registered under a different name variation or may have been marked inactive. Checking both tabs ensures you find existing records and avoid duplicates.

Creating a New Person Record

If no records match your search and you are confident that the individual does not already exist in your database, you can create a new person record.

Step 1   Click New Person Registration

At the top of the Person Registry, click the + New Person Registration button. This will open the person creation form.

Click the “+ New Person Registration” button to begin creating a new record.

Step 2   Fill Out the Identity Section

The creation form opens with the Identity section at the top. Fill out all available fields. At minimum, the following required fields must be completed before saving. Required fields are identified by an asterisk (*) on the form.

  • Legal First Name
  • Legal Last Name
  • Date of Birth

Additional identity fields may include middle names, preferred name, pronouns, gender identity, legal gender, care card number, band ID, MCFD file number, and immigration status.

The Identity section of the person creation form with required fields highlighted.

Step 3   Complete Additional Sections

Below the Identity section, you can expand and fill out additional profile sections:

  • Contact & Communication – phone numbers, email address
  • Address & Location – address type, street address, postal code
  • Indigenous Identity & Community – Indigenous identification, community details

Additional profile sections: Contact & Communication, Address & Location, and Indigenous Identity & Community.

Step 4   Save the Record

Once all required fields are complete, click the Save button in the bottom-right corner of the form. The system will check if both the name and date of birth match an existing record. If a potential duplicate is detected, those results will display for you to select from instead of creating a new entry.

Required fields completed and ready to save.

Duplicate detection:

Mareto automatically checks for duplicates when you save. If both the name and date of birth match an existing record, those results will be displayed for you to select from. This prevents accidental duplicate entries in your system.

Quick Reference

Required Fields for New Person Records

FieldSectionNotes
Legal First NameIdentityUsed for duplicate detection
Legal Last NameIdentityUsed for duplicate detection
Date of BirthIdentityUsed for duplicate detection

Search Methods

Search ByExample
First nameNicole
Last nameCroft
Last name, first nameCroft, Nicole
Date of birth01/15/1990
Alias / other namesAny known name variation

Key Reminders

  • Always search the Person Registry before creating a new record
  • Check both Active and Inactive record tabs during your search
  • Complete all required fields before saving
  • The Person Module is shared across all programs — one record per person
  • Contact information updated in one program is reflected across all programs
